title: Next Level YouTube Editor WANTED
description:
— WHO WE ARE —…



We are a small group of friends looking to make our mark on the YouTube landscape, with our action-packed and humorous gameplay and challenges.



Check out our channel: 



— WHAT WE‘RE LOOKING FOR —



We’re looking for remote, highly experienced Video Editors who aspire to be the best YouTube editors in the world. Period.



If hired, you will spend the majority of your time editing and animating fast-paced, highly engaging YouTube videos (our style could be compared to Game Theory, MortarMarv, or Finzar).



Our team culture is always supportive and encouraging, but you should also expect to be held to an exceptionally high standard because that’s how we become the best in the world at what we do (If you hate getting lots of revisions/feedback on your work, you won’t fit in here).



Your day-to-day will also involve analyzing previous videos' Viewer Retention Analytics Graphs to uncover opportunities to improve future edits.



— THE IDEAL CANDIDATE —



Here are the main skills and abilities we’re looking for in our applicants:



VIDEO EDITING (Required): Obviously, you must be an exceptionally talented video editor from a technical perspective. However, you must also have the passion, creativity, and sense of humor to be able to turn bland, average footage into highly engaging videos that educate AND entertain viewers.



ANIMATION (Required): You must be capable of creating high-quality 2d overlay, titling, and keyframing animations that visually illustrate the concepts we discuss in our videos.



— YOUR KPIs —



Your performance will be graded using the following KPIs:



1 Good Audience Retention, which we’ll be able to measure and learn from through our YouTube analytics.



2 The number of revisions the videos you edit require before being finalised (the less, the better)



3 The number of hours it takes you to create a video (the less, the better)



— WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU —



Here are just some of the areas where you’ll have the opportunity to learn and grow at our channel.



STABLE & SECURE: This is a very stable and secure job. Our most senior editors have been working with us full-time for 3+ years. No more wishy-washy freelance gigs.



LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES: If you prove yourself and we continue with our current growth rate, you will likely be presented with senior opportunities down the track.



LEARN FROM THE BEST IN THE WORLD: With us, you get to learn from people who are constantly striving to be the best in the world. We’re supportive and encouraging of one another, but we also hold each other to very high standards.



CREATIVE FREEDOM: Once you’ve mastered our style, we encourage outside-the-box thinking. We’re always open to experimenting with new techniques and styles, and then we analyze the data to see how viewers react.



FLEXIBLE WORK HOURS: You can work whenever you want within reason; however, we expect you to be available for tea
etings (We are based in California, and tea
etings are usually in the late evening PT).



— TO APPLY —



If you're interested, please submit the following form (No need for a CV or cover letter. It’s not the 1900s anymore :D):






Please note: All successful job applications will start with a virtual interview and trial project/s to make sure that we’re the right fit for each other.



Thank you for your consideration, and best of luck on your job search!

title: Youtube Video Editor for Marketing Edutainment Channel
description:
I run a marketing-focused YouTube channel where I talk directly to camera about marketing strategy, AI, branding, and business growth. I'm looking for a skilled video editor who can turn my raw talking-head footage into polished, engaging, high-retention videos, the kind that educate and entertain at the same time.



This is not a "just cut out the pauses" role. I need an editor who can bring energy to the footage through motion graphics, on-screen text, B-roll inserts, data callouts, and smart pacing. Think edutainment, not lecture.

Reference Channels (Please Watch Before Applying)



Study these three channels closely, they represent the editing style I'm going for:

- Wes McDowell, 
 (clean talking-head editing with smooth B-roll inserts, branded graphics, and a professional but approachable feel)

- Adam Erhart, 
 (high energy, quick cuts, bold on-screen text, strong transitions, motion graphics that reinforce key points)

- Exposure Ninja, 
 (excellent use of charts, data visualizations, screen recordings, animated diagrams, and B-roll to keep viewers engaged through educational content)



What all three have in common (and what I want): talking-head footage brought to life with kinetic text, motion graphics, relevant B-roll/screenshots, data callouts when stats are mentioned, clear section transitions, and pacing that holds attention without feeling frantic.



What You'll Be Doing

- Editing 6-12 minute talking-head YouTube videos (raw footage provided)

- Adding on-screen text and kinetic typography to reinforce key points

- Creating or sourcing relevant B-roll, screenshots, and visual inserts when I reference people, brands, platforms, or data

- Designing simple motion graphics (charts, diagrams, stat callouts)

- Adding background music, sound design, and audio normalization

- Clean jump cuts to maintain energy and remove dead air

- Applying light color grading (warm, natural, not cinematic or flat)

- Flagging thumbnail-worthy frames from the footage

- Delivering 1–2 videos per week (volume may grow)



Required Skills

- Proficient in FinalCut 

- After Effects (or equivalent) for motion graphics and text animations

- Strong understanding of YouTube retention editing, you know why viewers click away and how to prevent it

- Ability to match an editing style from reference videos

- Good eye for typography, layout, and visual hierarchy

- Reliable internet and ability to handle large video files



Nice to Have

- Experience editing marketing or business YouTube content

- Thumbnail design skills (Photoshop/Canva)

- Familiarity with Canva, Descript, or CapCut as supplementary tools

- Understanding of YouTube SEO (titles, descriptions, chapters)



What I'm NOT Looking For

- Generic "cut the pauses" editing with no visual flair

- Overly cinematic or movie-trailer style editing

- Heavy use of stock footage or AI-generated visuals

- Trendy editing gimmicks (subway surfers splits, excessive 
 inserts)



How to Apply

Please include in your application:

1. A link to your portfolio or 2–3 YouTube videos you've edited (marketing/business content preferred)

2. Watch one video from each of the three reference channels above and tell me in 2–3 sentences what you notice about their editing style

3. Your rate (per video or monthly) and availability

4. A short Loom video (1–2 min) introducing yourself and why you'd be a good fit



Start your message with the word "EDUTAINMENT" so I know you read the full posting.



Compensation: Open to discussion based on experience. Looking for a long-term partnership, not a one-off gig. Part-time to start with potential to grow.

title: Full-Time UI/Product Designer for Software Company - Long-Term Role
description:
Hi! We're a software and AI company based in the US. We build B2B and B2C web apps, mobile apps, browser extensions and desktop applications. We're looking for a full-time designer to join our team long-term.



This is not a freelance gig. I'm looking for someone who wants a stable, ongoing role where you grow with the company and become an essential part of the team.



The design work includes:

• Website design (landing pages, marketing pages)

• Dashboard and web app UI design

• Mobile app design (including onboarding flows)

• App Store and Google Play listing graphics

• Desktop application installer design

• Icons, social media graphics, and marketing materials

• Other product listing visuals (Chrome Web Store, Google Marketplace)





I'll provide detailed training on our brand, style, and processes. I do a lot of this design work myself right now, so I can teach you exactly how I think about it and how I do it. What I need from you is strong design fundamentals and a willingness to learn my approach.

title: Paid Ads Specialist (Google & Meta)
description:
Paid Ads Specialist (GooJob Title: Performance Marketing Manager

 Location: Remote

 Job Type: Full-time

About Wandering Lion

Wandering Lion is a strategy-led growth studio helping founder-led businesses build premium brands, install scalable growth systems, and generate measurable commercial outcomes — without chaos. We do not sell marketing activity for the sake of it. We build structured growth systems that connect brand, performance, systems, and reporting to real business results.

Our model is disciplined: foundation first, then build, then scale. That means we care deeply about clarity, accountability, and making sure paid media works inside a stronger commercial system — not in isolation.

The Role

We are looking for a commercially sharp Performance Marketing Manager to lead campaign execution, optimisation, and channel development across Google Ads, Meta Ads, and other current or emerging paid acquisition platforms.

This role is not limited to today’s core channels. As the business grows, this position may also support expansion into additional advertising ecosystems, marketing technology, AI-enabled tools, automation platforms, and other digital growth systems that improve performance, reporting, and operational efficiency.

This is not a button-pushing media buying role. We want someone who understands that ads amplify clarity — they do not create it — and who can help translate strategy into efficient, measurable growth.

You will work across founder-led brands that need more than clicks and impressions. They need disciplined media execution, better conversion performance, clearer reporting, and someone who understands the commercial outcome behind the spend. Our ideal clients value structure, proof, and accountability, so your work must reflect that standard.

Key Responsibilities

Paid media strategy and planning

 Build channel strategies across Google, Meta, and other relevant paid platforms based on client goals, audience behaviour, customer journey stage, and conversion priorities.

Campaign setup and execution

 Launch, manage, and optimise campaigns across search, display, remarketing, paid social, and future paid channels where appropriate.

Audience, keyword, and market research

 Conduct keyword research, audience segmentation, competitor review, and targeting refinement to improve efficiency and lead quality.

Creative and messaging alignment

 Work closely with strategy and creative to ensure ad messaging reflects the brand, strengthens trust, and supports conversion.

Testing and optimisation

 Run structured tests across headlines, copy, creative, offers, landing pages, audience segments, and funnel stages to improve performance over time.

Budget and bid management

 Allocate spend carefully, adjust bids based on performance, and manage budgets with commercial discipline.

Performance reporting

 Translate campaign and funnel data into clear reporting that explains what happened, what it means, and what we do next.

Technology and AI adoption

 Help identify and implement useful tools, AI software, automation workflows, and platform enhancements that improve campaign performance, efficiency, internal reporting, or client delivery.

Conversion and systems thinking

 Monitor lead quality, conversion paths, attribution, and funnel performance — not just platfor
trics — to support real business outcomes.

What We’re Looking For

Proven experience managing Google Ads and Meta Ads accounts with measurable results

Strong understanding of paid media fundamentals, attribution, conversion tracking, and optimisation

Confidence learning and adapting to new advertising platforms, AI tools, and digital systems as the role evolves

Ability to think beyond platfor
trics and connect spend to commercial outcomes

Strong communication skills and the ability to explain performance clearly

A structured, accountable, detail-driven approach to campaign management

Comfort working in a business that values quality over volume and clarity over noise

Preferred

Experience across service-based businesses, eCommerce, trades, or founder-led brands

Familiarity with landing page optimisation and CRO principles

Experience using AI tools, automation platforms, reporting dashboards, or workflow software to improve marketing operations

Google Ads certifications

Meta advertising certifications

Experience using CRM, analytics, and reporting platforms

What Success Looks Like in This Role

Campaigns are well-structured, well-managed, and commercially aligned

Reporting is clear, useful, and trusted by clients

Paid media performance improves through disciplined testing and optimisation

Useful tools and systems are introduced where they genuinely improve efficiency or outcomes

Clients feel confident that spend is being managed with accountability

You contribute to a premium client experience, not just campaign delivery

Why Join Wandering Lion

You’ll be joining a business built around calm authority, commercial clarity, and human confidence. We are not trying to become a high-volume agency. We are building a quality-protected growth studio that integrates brand, performance, and systems under one roof.

That means:

You’ll work on higher-quality engagements

You’ll be part of a strategy-led model, not random execution

You’ll collaborate with a team that values standards, structure, and outcomes

You’ll help build growth systems that genuinely move businesses forward

Before We Proceed

Before we potentially move to the interview stage, we’d like to gather a few details to make sure the role is a strong fit and that you’ll be set up for success working remotely with our team.

1. PC / Laptop specifications + internet speed

 Please share your device specifications, including CPU, RAM, and storage, along with a recent internet speed test result. Reliable performance and strong internet are essential for day-to-day work.

2. Current employment or side work

 Are you currently employed or doing any freelance or side work? We are open to offshore tea
mbers having multiple roles. What matters most to us is transparency, so we understand your typical schedule and can support you properly.

3. Hubstaff tracker

 Are you comfortable using Hubstaff time tracking? Our whole team operates remotely, including our field and admin staff in Australia. Hubstaff helps us understand time allocation across projects, improve internal reporting, and provide clearer updates to clients.

4. Working hours / availability

 Can you commit to the required hours? Our standard workdays are Monday to Friday, generally within 9:00am–5:00pm Philippines time. We offer reasonable flexibility — family and life come first — but we do need consistency and clear communication.

5. Expected salary (PHP)

 If this differs from what was listed in the advert, please confirm your expected salary in PHP.

6. CV / Resume

 Please attach your CV if you have not already done so.

7. Relevant training / courses

 Please include any relevant training, certifications, or courses you have completed.

8. English level

 Please confirm your English level (C1 or C2) and your confidence in both written and spoken English.

Team Context

For context, we currently have a team of 10 people in the Philippines, all working from home, and we are continuing to grow. We value honesty, transparency, and teamwork. We are looking for people who are humble, reliable, and easy to work with — because every role matters, and the most important thing is a happy team doing work they enjoy, surrounded by like-minded people.

How to Apply

Please send your CV, a short introduction, and responses to the questions above, along with 2–3 examples of accounts, campaigns, platforms, or systems you have worked on and the commercial outcomes achieved.

Thank you. We look forward to hearing from you.





gle & Meta)

title: Telecom Rollout Specialist.  Telecom tech leader for set up
description:
We use ring central and need someone to set up the system over again for us.

Our phones are set up to route to the next of 3 offices if the first office cannot pick up.  This is backfiring.

We need someone who can 

1) remove that rollover function 

2) Set up "This call is being recorded for quality assurance....please let us know if you'd prefer us to call you back on a non recorded line."

3) Set up routing: "Press 1 if you'd like to explore services for your family,.  Press 2 if you'd like to talk to our Scheduling Department.  Press 3 if you'ld like to talk to a RECRUITER about becoming a caregiver.  Press 4 to dial by first or last name.

4) Set up optional choice to leave a message or to dial zero to be connected back to a live person

5) Set up assurances that voi
s are notified to people via
.

6) Set up ranking for how soon someone would like a call back (within 20 minutes, within two hours, before office staff go home, next day)

7) Then take the prerecorded soundbites I've done and make hold time "Music" spliced with them as I direct over time.



Then we have to collect everyone's Verizon cell phones, download ring central and teach them how to use it.  We need to prevent them from  calling out via their verizon cell number ever again.  There are 18 staff.  You likely will require individual appointments back to back to set up  each cell phone with them via
 correctly and train them how to make their own number invisible.  You'd need to talk to verizon about how to do that.



We'd like all this done within a matter of weeks.

- Klaviyo (light use for
 assets)

- Meta and TikTok Ads Manager (view for performance insights)



Requirements:

- At least 2 years of experience in graphic design or creative production

- Strong portfolio of ad creatives (social media or e-commerce)

- Proficiency in Canva, Photoshop, and video editing tools

- Experience working with Shopify (product pages, basic site updates)

- Understanding of what makes ad creatives convert

- Ability to work fast and iterate based on feedback

- Strong attention to detail and design skills

- Comfortable in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

- Excellent English communication skills



Strongly Preferred:

- Experience in regulated industries

- Motion graphics or advanced video editing skills

- Basic knowledge of UX/UI or conversion rate optimization (CRO)

- Experience with Webflow or landing page builders

- Familiarity with AI tools for creative production

- Understanding of direct-response marketing



If you are a creative and performance-driven designer who enjoys producing content that converts, we’d love to hear from you.



APPLY NOW!

title: YouTube Gaming Video Editor
description:
— WHO WE ARE —…



We are a small group of friends looking to make our mark on the YouTube landscape, with our action-packed and humorous gameplay and challenges.



Check out our channel: 



— WHAT WE‘RE LOOKING FOR —



We’re looking for remote, highly experienced Video Editors who aspire to be the best YouTube editors in the world. Period.



If hired, you will spend the majority of your time editing and animating fast-paced, highly engaging YouTube videos (our style could be compared to Game Theory, MortarMarv, or Finzar).



Our team culture is always supportive and encouraging, but you should also expect to be held to an exceptionally high standard because that’s how we become the best in the world at what we do (If you hate getting lots of revisions/feedback on your work, you won’t fit in here).



Your day-to-day will also involve analyzing previous videos' Viewer Retention Analytics Graphs to uncover opportunities to improve future edits.



— THE IDEAL CANDIDATE —



Here are the main skills and abilities we’re looking for in our applicants:



VIDEO EDITING (Required): Obviously, you must be an exceptionally talented video editor from a technical perspective. However, you must also have the passion, creativity, and sense of humor to be able to turn bland, average footage into highly engaging videos that educate AND entertain viewers.



ANIMATION (Required): You must be capable of creating high-quality 2d overlay, titling, and keyframing animations that visually illustrate the concepts we discuss in our videos.



— YOUR KPIs —



Your performance will be graded using the following KPIs:



1 Good Audience Retention, which we’ll be able to measure and learn from through our YouTube analytics. 



2 The number of revisions the videos you edit require before being finalised (the less, the better)



3 The number of hours it takes you to create a video (the less, the better)



— WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU —



Here are just some of the areas where you’ll have the opportunity to learn and grow at our channel.



STABLE & SECURE: This is a very stable and secure job. Our most senior editors have been working with us full-time for 3+ years. No more wishy-washy freelance gigs.



LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES: If you prove yourself and we continue with our current growth rate, you will likely be presented with senior opportunities down the track.



LEARN FROM THE BEST IN THE WORLD: With us, you get to learn from people who are constantly striving to be the best in the world. We’re supportive and encouraging of one another, but we also hold each other to very high standards.



CREATIVE FREEDOM: Once you’ve mastered our style, we encourage outside-the-box thinking. We’re always open to experimenting with new techniques and styles, and then we analyze the data to see how viewers react.



FLEXIBLE WORK HOURS: You can work whenever you want within reason; however, we expect you to be available for tea
etings (We are based in California, and tea
etings are usually in the late evening PT).



— TO APPLY —



If you're interested, please submit the following form (No need for a CV or cover letter. It’s not the 1900s anymore :D): 







Please note: All successful job applications will start with a virtual interview and trial project/s to make sure that we’re the right fit for each other.



Thank you for your consideration, and best of luck on your job search!

title: Product Information and Data Entry Specialist
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented Product Information & Data Entry Specialist to create, organize, and maintain accurate product information for a home goods company. This role involves more than basic data entry, as it requires strong attention to detail, sound judgment, and the ability to describe products clearly in a way that supports both customer understanding and internal organization. 



The ideal candidate can review product details, specifications, and images, then enter and refine product descriptions, attributes, and related information with consistency and accuracy. Responsibilities include creating and updating product descriptions, entering product data into internal systems, organizing product information for easy reference, maintaining accuracy across product records, and helping ensure products are presented in a clear and structured way for both internal teams and customers. Prior experience with product data entry, catalog management, product descriptions, or similar product information work is preferred.



Must apply using this link,

title: Hiring Video Editor for YouTube Business (Experience Required)
description:
We are looking for a a video editor who is able to effectively use CapCut and Canva to create YouTube videos matching our existing style by following training provided.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Upload a video explaining why you are fit for this role and explain your previous experience, especially with CapCut and Canva

- You must have experience with using CapCut and Canva

- Ideally you already have a CapCut Pro account



We have many channels that we run, so there is opportunity for increased workload or a monthly rate as we expand. 



Please ensure you have read the requirements prior to applying, I look forward to hearing from you.

title: Video Editor (Part-Time, Long-Term)
description:
I'm a solo video and photo producer based in the US. I work with ongoing clients across real estate, ministry/leadership, and small business — shooting and producing content that actually moves the needle for the people I serve. It's a lean, quality-first operation. The work you do matters, and you're not one of a hundred editors in a queue.

I have consistent, recurring volume. If you do good work, you stay busy. If you're great, this role grows with you.



THE ROLE

I'm looking for a part-time video editor to start, with a real path to full-time as volume and trust grow. This is a long-term hire — not a gig. I want someone who shows up consistently, executes to a standard, and builds something together over time.

Priority #1 from day one: Editing and full content packaging for an ongoing YouTube and social media channel — weekly teaching and leadership content for pastors and church leaders. Consistent, recurring work. You'll own it end to end.

Priority #2: Real estate video editing — cinematic property walkthrough videos for residential and rural/land listings.



WHAT YOU'LL BE EDITING

HNI YouTube & Social Content — Primary, starts immediately

This is a teaching/leadership channel for church leaders and ministry teams. Clean, professional talking-head content with minimal, purposeful editing. Volume: 4 YouTube videos + 4 Reels per month.

Footage delivered via Dropbox. Files published to YouTube and
 through my systems.

Your full deliverable for every long-form video:

EDIT (Adobe Premiere Pro):



A-roll trimming, color correction, audio mastering, and minimal on-screen text/titles

Clean, professional style — purposeful cuts, not flashy

Detailed editing SOPs and style references provided when you start



DELIVERY PACKAGE:



Finished video export

Original .prproj file with all assets

Thumbnail expression stills

Uploaded to Dropbox and shared via link



CONTENT PACKAGE (AI-assisted via Claude — you generate options, I make final calls):



3 title options — use Claude to generate, submit your 3 best for my final selection

YouTube description (SEO-aware, includes chapter markers)

Tags/keywords

3 Canva thumbnail options — using expression stills, branded text overlay — I choose the best one



Detailed SOPs and Claude prompts for this workflow will be provided.

Your deliverable for each Reel/Short:



30-90 seconds clipped from long-form — strong hook + clear payoff

Vertical format (9:16), captions added, music if appropriate

Ready to post on YouTube Shorts +
 Reels





Real Estate Video — Secondary, consistent volume

Cinematic property walkthrough edits for residential homes and rural/land listings.



Average length: ~2 minutes

Style: quality-focused, not flashy — good music, smooth color grade, clean pacing

Footage shot on Sony cameras, S-Log 3 with Phantom LUTS — color correction from log required

Drone/aerial footage integration is common

Deliver finished export + original .prproj file every time





Additional Project Types (grows over time):



Church and event recap videos

Client brand and promo videos

Short-form social content

Personal brand YouTube content





SKILLS & REQUIREMENTS

Required:



Adobe Premiere Pro — all edits happen here.

S-Log 3 / log footage color correction — Sony cameras, Phantom LUTS used

Audio processing fundamentals — gain, compression, hard limiting

Canva — thumbnail creation with text overlays and branded design

Claude (Anthropic) — I work out of Claude for all AI-assisted tasks. Comfortable using it required; prior Claude experience is a strong plus

Deliver both a finished export and the original .prproj file for every project, every time

Dropbox delivery for everything

Comfortable working across multiple project types and styles



Strongly Preferred:



YouTube editing experience (talking-head/teaching content)

Short-form Reels/Shorts editing — vertical format, captions, clipping long-form

Real estate or property video editing experience

Drone/aerial footage integration



Nice to Have:



Experience with Dehancer





PAY



$5-$10/hr depending on experience and demonstrated skill

Higher skill = higher starting rate — if you're genuinely good, I'll pay for it

This is not a ceiling. Rate and hours grow as you prove yourself.

The goal is full-time. That's where this heads if you deliver.





TURNAROUND



Standard: 3-5 business days per project

Consistent on-time delivery is one of the most important things I track





TIME TRACKING & INTRODUCTORY PERIOD



TimeProof required to start — protects both of us while we build trust

The first 2-3 months are an introductory period — performance evaluated on task completion, quality, communication, and overall fit

If it's a good fit, this becomes a long-term role with growing hours and rate





COMMUNICATION



Primary communication via WhatsApp — day-to-day updates, feedback, and check-ins all happen there. You need to have it and use it.

Responsive and reliable — not 24/7, but timely

Occasional video calls will happen — be comfortable on camera with clear English

Written English fluency required. Ask questions early.





WHO I'M LOOKING FOR

The technical skills matter. But these things matter just as much:



Overdeliver. Don't just do what's expected — go a little beyond and wow people with something they're not even paying for. Value the relationship more than the transaction.

Be a pleasure to work with. It's not just the final product people are paying for — it's the entire process from A to Z. Be a human being that people genuinely enjoy working with. This matters even more working remotely.

Enjoy what you do. You only do well what you love. If editing feels like a chore, this isn't the right fit.

Be efficient. A lot of people who are less talented get more work because they turn things around on time and don't procrastinate. Deadlines are not suggestions.

Be precise. Every revision note needs to be followed — fully, the first time. Missing the same note twice is a bigger deal than most people think. This is the one I take most seriously.

Set expectations, then meet and exceed them. If something's going to be late or you're unsure — say so early. I'd rather know than be surprised.



I'm not looking for the most experienced editor in the world. I'm looking for someone hungry, skilled, and genuinely enjoyable to work with. Personality matters just as much as the work.



THE HIRING PROCESS



Apply with your portfolio or reel — YouTube and/or real estate samples are a strong plus

Top candidates selected based on portfolio and application quality

Brief written or video screening with finalists

Paid test edit for final candidates — you'll receive raw footage and a reference cut to match





EXAMPLE WORK

Take a look at these before applying — this is exactly what you'd be editing:

HNI YouTube (primary — the channel you'd be editing for):



Teaching/leadership content for pastors and church leaders. Talking-head/podcast style, desk setup. Mix of long-form (45min-1hr+) and shorter clips (8-20min). Bold text-heavy thumbnails, curiosity-driven titles. Study a few videos before applying.

Real estate videos (secondary workload):



Land, hunting tracts, luxury properties, rural/residential across Georgia and Virginia. Cinematic, quality-focused.

My personal YouTube channel (style reference):





HOW TO APPLY

Use the subject line "Video Editor Application" — applications without this will not be reviewed.

Include:



Link to your portfolio or reel (required — no link, not reviewed)

Any YouTube, real estate, or social media editing samples

Brief overview of your Premiere Pro, S-Log 3/color, and Canva experience

Your timezone and availability



Answer ALL of the following questions:



Where in the Philippines are you?

How are power outages where you live?

Do you have your own computer? What are the specs?

How fast is your internet connection?

Do you have another job? If so, where and how many hours per week?

How long have you been doing video editing?

What have you done in the last 12 months to improve your editing skills?

What's the best piece of work you've ever done — and why did it turn out so well?

How long would you expect to work with me if you get this role?

Do you have any other skills not listed that might be useful?



Final required question (answer this last):

"Describe one editing project where you had to match a specific style that wasn't your own. How did you approach it, and what was the result?"

Applications that skip the questions will not be reviewed.

Remote position. Strong preference for availability overlapping US Eastern or Central business hours.

title: AI VIDEO EDITOR WANTED — SKINCARE BRAND
description:
???? AI VIDEO EDITOR WANTED — SKINCARE BRAND





We're a fast-growing skincare brand creating premium AI-generated video content — and we need someone who can keep up.



MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:

? 5–8 videos per day

? 6 days per week

? Full-time availability (8hrs/day)

? Fast turnaround. Fast communication. No excuses.



YOU MUST BE SKILLED IN:

? Generating high-quality images with Higgsfield AI

? Turning visuals into videos using Veo 3

? Voice and avatar work with ElevenLabs & HeyGen

? Prompt engineering that actually converts



WE'RE LOOKING FOR EDITORS WHO ARE:

— AI-native (not beginners figuring it out on our time)

— Fast, efficient, and creatively sharp

— Obsessed with quality and detail

— Comfortable in the beauty/skincare space



This is full-time. This is real work. This is for people who are already doing this at a high level.



???? TO APPLY:

Send your most recent AI-generated videos only.

No resumes. No regular edits. No intros.

We hire based on your actual work.

title: Social Media Content Specialist (VA)
description:
???? Social Media Content Specialist (VA) – Fitness Brand



We’re a fast-growing fitness business based in Australia, and we’re looking for a high-quality Content Specialist to take ownership of our social media execution.



This is not a basic VA role.

We want someone who can take direction and turn it into engaging, high-performing content... consistently.



???? What You’ll Be Responsible For

Editing short-form video content (Reels / TikTok style)

Selecting the best clips from raw footage

Adding captions, hooks, pacing, and structure to videos

Writing captions in our brand voice

Scheduling and posting content



???? Content Output

You will be responsible for:

Personal brand: 4 posts per week

Business Brand: 8–10 posts per week



???? Total: 12–14 posts per week



All content direction will be provided via a 30-day content planner.



???? How We Work

We film and provide raw content

You take that content and turn it into finished, polished posts

You manage posting and ensure consistency across both accounts

This role is about execution at a high level, not just following basic instructions.





???? Editing Standard (Important)

We are looking for someone who can;

Create clean, engaging edits (not basic cuts)

Use strong pacing and flow

Add captions that are easy to read and engaging

Understand hooks and attention-grabbing formats

Replicate trending styles when relevant



???? If your edits feel basic, this role is not for you.



?? Working Structure

Flexible working hours

Must ensure all content is edited, scheduled, and posted on time

Strong organisation and consistency is critical



???? Compensation

Mid-range monthly salary (based on experience)

Opportunity for long-term growth within the business



? What We’re Looking For

Strong experience editing short-form video (Reels / TikTok)

Ability to take raw footage and turn it into quality content

Good understanding of social media trends and formats

Strong attention to detail

Ability to match tone and style of an existing brand

Reliable, consistent, and accountable



???? Not For You If

You only do basic edits

You need step-by-step instructions for every post

You struggle with consistency or deadlines



???? Why This Role Is Different

We already have:

A clear content strategy

A strong brand voice

A high volume of content



We just need the right person to execute at a high level consistently.





???? How To Apply

Send your application to 
 directly with;



Examples of short-form videos you’ve edited 

Links to accounts you’ve worked on 

A short video (1–2 mins) explaining your editing process & a bit about you

Your favourite piece of content you’ve created and why (and share it with us)



If you’re confident you can take raw content and turn it into engaging, high-quality posts consistently, we want to hear from you.

title: AI Content Operator & Video Editor (Short-Form / Growth-Focused)
description:
We run a fast-growing online health company helping people get out of pain and move better without relying on traditional healthcare. We create a lot of content (short-form videos, ads, educational content), and we’re looking for someone who can help us turn 1 video into 10–30 high-performing pieces of content using AI.



This is NOT a basic video editing job. This is NOT just dropping a video into OpusClip (or other similar tool).

We are looking for someone who understands content, attention, and performance. Great English communication is required.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

Take raw video footage and turn it into:

- Short-form videos (Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)

- Editing Long form videos (youtube)

- Adding AI b-roll or creating AI generated videos based on a script. 

- Ad creatives

- Create multiple variations of the same content (different hooks, captions, pacing)



Use AI tools to:

- Rewrite hooks and scripts

- Generate new content ideas

- Add captions, fast cuts, zooms, and edits that improve retention



TOOLS YOU SHOULD BE FAMILIAR WITH:

- Descript

- CapCut/Premiere Pro/Final Cut

- Claude/ChatGPT or similar for scripting + ideation

- HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Nano Banana, Opus, or AI content tools



THIS JOB IS NOT FOR YOU IF:

- You don’t use AI tools regularly

- You only know how to edit but don’t understand content performance

- You need step-by-step instructions for everything

- You focus on making things “look pretty” instead of performing well



THIS JOB IS FOR YOU IF:

- You enjoy breaking down viral videos and recreating them

- You like testing different hooks and ideas

- You want to grow with a company long-term

- You’re excited about AI and using it to work faster and smarter



If you're interested, add the words, Orange Cactus, to the end of your
.



Hope to hear from you!

title: Real Estate Sales - Operations - Virtual Assistant
description:
We are hiring a high-performance Real Estate Virtual Assistant focused on outbound prospecting, CRM management, and operational support.



Extremely good English speaking sills required. 



This role is:



40% Outbound Calling & Follow-Up

40?min & CRM Management

20% Financial & Operational Coordination



This is a performance-based role with bonuses tied directly to appointments and closed transactions.



Core Responsibilities

1- Outbound Calling & Lead Generation (40%)

100 calls per day (FSBO, expired listings, geographic farming, database follow-up)

Qualify seller motivation

Identify warm leads

Book listing appointments

Log detailed call notes in Follow Up Boss

Maintain pipeline integrity



Weekly Targets:

5 warm leads

Minimum 1 qualified appointment



Tools:

Mojo Dialer

Follow Up Boss CRM



2- Admin & CRM Management (40%)

Calendar & inbox management

Follow-up scheduling

Database tagging & cleanup

Digital signature coordination

Transaction checklist management

Maintain zero CRM backlog

Success Metric:

No missed follow-ups

Clean and organized CRM at all times





3- Financial & Operations Support (20%)

Categorizing transactions in QuickBooks Online

Monthly reconciliation

Tracking rental/property performance

Preparing simple cash flow & P&L summaries

KPIs & Performance Metrics



Daily

100+ outbound calls

All notes logged in CRM



Weekly

5 warm leads

1 qualified appointment



Monthly

Financial reconciliation complete

CRM fully updated

Bonus review



Compensation

$3/4/hour base

$25 per qualified appointment set

$ Bi Annual Bonus $
USD 

$700 per closed transaction



Top performers can substantially increase income through performance bonuses.





Ideal Candidate:



Excellent spoken English

Comfortable with cold calling and objection handling

Extremely organized and detail-oriented

CRM disciplined

Familiar with QuickBooks Online

Self-directed with minimal supervision





You will be ahead if you submit a voice recording about yourself and role play:





Submit a short video (or audio if more comfortable) assessment (2 to 5 minutes max) .

Please record yourself answering the following:

1- Introduce yourself and describe your cold calling experience.

2- Roleplay this situation:

You are calling a homeowner in one of these three scenarios (chose one):

-Homeowner whose listing expired

-Homeowner in Manhattan Beach that lived there since 1965

-Executor of a testate real estate (owner passed away, calling heirs)



Introduce yourself as working for Shoreview Partners, Real Estate brokerage out of Manhattan Beach. Your goal is to qualify them as potential clients, and ultimately set an appointment to walk the property an provide some value (apprise estimate, upgrades, off market sale...)



They say:

"We're not interested. We're just going to wait before selling again."



Respond naturally as if on the phone.





3- Tell us what success in this role would look like for you in 12 months, and how many cold calls have you made in a single day before?



Please submit your video or audio via Google Drive link here

title: Full-Time Video Editor (For Short-form Content)
description:
If you love editing engaging TikToks/Instagram Reels, this is the perfect role for you! 



We’re looking for a full-time video editor to join our fast-growing team and help create content that generates millions of views on social media every month.



==About Us==



Flowlync is a social media marketing agency that helps business owners grow their personal brands online and grow their business. We handle the full content production process from ideation to editing and posting. 



We’re growing quickly and are looking for talented editors who want to be part of a high-performance team and grow within the company long-term. 



==What You’ll Be Doing==



As a Video Editor, you will:



• Edit short-form talking-head videos for social media (TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts)

• Turn raw recordings from clients into engaging edited videos

• Work with clear daily deadlines and deliverables

• Manage editing for a set group of clients

• Collaborate with our team to improve content performance



==Requirements==



To be considered, you must:



• Be available full-time (40 hours per week)

title: Talent Manager | UGC Creator Finder
description:
Howdy! We are looking for a Talent Manager to help us find and recruit UGC creators for our brand.



This role is all about having a sharp eye for talent. You'll be scrolling TikTok, Instagram, and using other platforms to identify micro influencers and creators that have the right energy, audience, and content style to produce high-converting UGC for us. We will train you on exactly who we're looking for, but you need to already have a solid understanding of the UGC space and what makes a creator actually effective at driving results.



You'll be tasked with:



- Sourcing and reaching out to UGC creators on TikTok and Instagram

- Sending outreach
s and negotiating deals to bring creators on board

- Onboarding new creators and coordinating product shipments

- Organizing content that have been delivered from creators

- Managing creator relationships

- Logging all creator information (pricing, contact details, post rates, etc.) into a Google Sheet

- Reporting daily on WhatsApp



We are looking for:



- A genuine understanding of the UGC space and what makes a creator convert, not just look good

- Strong negotiation and bargaining skills: you'll be agreeing on rates and deals regularly

- Highly responsive and a great communicator! Daily check-ins and clear reporting are a big part of this role

- Organized and detail-oriented: our creator database needs to be clean and up to date at all times



Of course, you will be compensated fairly :)



We give bonuses every time you successfully onboard a new creator: the more you bring in, the more you earn. 

We also provide a clear path to a senior talent role with an increased salary for people who consistently deliver.

You will NEVER be overworked. If you have personal plans or need time off, we will always respect it, just let us know beforehand!



Begin your application with the word APPLE as your very first word. Keep your entire application to 5 sentences or less. 



In addition to your application, find 5 American, female micro influencers on Instagram (less than 30,000 followers) that would be suitable to create content for the brand "Javvy Coffee." Link them in your application. Applications without this portion will be immediately denied.



Looking forward to having you as part of our team.

title: Operations & Sales Growth Executive Assistant
description:
About Us

The Event Engineer helps entrepreneurs scale to 7-figures and beyond. Led by Lisa Barry, we build high-converting event systems and world-class client experiences. Our mission is helping visionary founders grow wealth that fuels meaningful change.



The Role

This is not a "task-taker" role; this is a high-level partnership. As the Executive Assistant, your primary mission is to protect Lisa’s time, energy, and focus. You are the gatekeeper of the brand and the architect of the founder's daily life. 



While you handle high-level EA duties, you will also manage the "backend" systems (GoHighLevel & ClickUp) and AI automations that keep the 7-figure engine running smoothly behind the scenes. You are the bridge between brand exposure and revenue.



---



1. Primary Responsibility: Executive Support & Gatekeeping



Zone of Genius Protection: You own Lisa’s calendar. You don’t just book meetings; you architect her week to ensure "Deep Work" for software development and high-level sales is never interrupted.

Inbox Zen & Triage: You are the first line of defense. You filter, label, and respond to 80% of incoming communication so Lisa only sees what truly requires her eyes.

High-EQ Representation: Whether in the DMs or via
, you represent a premium brand. You interact with high-level clients and partners with warmth, professionalism, and absolute precision.

The Digital Housekeeper: You ensure Lisa’s digital environment (Google Drive, Slack, Inbox) is organized, clutter-free, and optimized for speed.





2. Secondary Responsibility: Systems & AI (Behind the Scenes)



ClickUp Command Center (Non-Negotiable): You manage the team’s workflow. You ensure the Creative Architect, Tech Guy, and Developer are on track, using ClickUp Automations to move tasks through to the "Wednesday Drop."

GHL Pipeline Mastery (Non-Negotiable): You ensure the CRM is 100?curate. You manage lead tags and nurture sequences, ensuring no "Golden Opportunity" ever falls through the cracks.

AI Integration: You leverage AI to make the business faster. This includes using AI for meeting transcriptions, instantly turning "voice notes" into formatted SOPs, and overseeing AI-driven lead responses.



---------------------------------------------------------------
. Who You Are



The Proactive Partner: You think three steps ahead. You solve problems before the founder knows they exist.

Systems-Minded: You get a dopamine hit from a clean CRM, a perfectly structured ClickUp board, and a zero-inbox.

Owner Mentality: You don't wait for a to-do list. You see a gap in the business and you build a bridge.

Flawless Communicator: Your written English is impeccable, persuasive, professional, and polished.



---------------------------------------------------------------
. Qualifications & Skills



2–3+ Years as an EA or Ops Manager for a high-ticket coach, consultant, or agency.

GoHighLevel (GHL) Proficiency: Experience managing leads, pipelines, and workflows.

ClickUp Experience: Ability to build and manage complex project boards and automations.

AI Literacy: Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Otter, etc.) to automate repetitive admin tasks.



---------------------------------------------------------------
. Application Requirements



Please provide the following to be considered for Phase 1:

1.  Resume: A brief history of your experience in operations and sales support.

2.  DISC Profile: Please include your results (D% I% S% C%).

3.  Rate your proficiency (1–10) with ClickUp, GoHighLevel, and AI tools.

4.  Tell us about one specific time you "saved the day" for a founder by fixing a system or managing a chaotic inbox.



---------------------------------------------------------------
. Work Arrangement & Compensation



Remote: 9am – 5pm AEDT (Australian Eastern Time)

Hours: 40 Hours / Week

Compensation: $1,000 AUD / month (Full-Time)

title: Instagram Virtual Assistant | Multiple Accounts | Full-Time Remote
description:
We are an established, profitable agency hiring a full-time Instagram Account Manager to handle 4 to 8+ accounts daily. We pay in USD, we have real structure, and we are building a team around the right people.



If you've been looking for a role where your work actually matters, where you're trusted with real responsibility, where you get paid properly and treated like a professional, keep reading.



This Is Not a Generic VA Role



We don't need someone to do random tasks. We need someone who can own multiple Instagram accounts, run them cleanly every day, and hold themselves to a high standard without being micromanaged.



We have SOPs. We have systems. We communicate well. You won't be left guessing what to do. But we expect you to show up, execute, and care about the quality of your work the same way we care about ours.



What Your Day Actually Looks Like



You log in and check your accounts. You review what needs to be posted, confirm the content matches the right account, format, and caption style. You post on schedule. You run through each account's engagement playbook: commenting, liking, story viewing, DM conversations. You check metrics and note anything that looks off. You log everything in your tracking sheet. You edit a few raw clips into Reels or stories. At the end of your shift, you send a clean report of what was done and what needs attention.



Then you do it again tomorrow. And the day after that. The people who thrive here are the ones who find satisfaction in doing consistent, high-quality work, not people who get bored after a week.



Your Responsibilities



· Manage 4 to 8+ Instagram accounts daily, each with its own branding, voice, and schedule

· Post the right content at the right time in the right format (Reels, stories, carousels, feed)

· Handle daily engagement: commenting, liking, story viewing, follow/unfollow based on each account's instructions

· Manage DMs across accounts, including scripted conversations that drive engagement

· Edit raw content into polished Instagram posts using CapCut, Canva, or similar

· Research competitors and trends to support content strategy

· Track all daily actions in Google Sheets

· Monitor performance metrics, flag issues, and suggest improvements

· Understand Instagram's content policies and know how to maximize reach without getting flagged

· Keep all account information, content, and business details strictly confidential



What You Need



· A solid computer or laptop (8GB+ RAM, SSD preferred). Everything runs from your computer. If your machine is slow, your work will be slow.

· Reliable internet (50Mbps+ download). If your connection drops regularly, this won't work.

· 6 to 8 hours per day, 5 to 6 days per week. We're flexible on timezone as long as your schedule is consistent and overlaps at least partially with US hours.

· Strong written English. You'll write captions, handle DMs, and send daily reports. Quality matters.

· Ability to follow detailed instructions the same way, every time, across multiple accounts.

· Comfort working with content that may not be suitable for all audiences. We'll discuss this further in the application.

· Real organizational skills. Managing 5+ accounts means 5+ sets of rules, 5+ posting schedules, 5+ brand voices. If that sounds overwhelming, this isn't the role. If it sounds like a challenge you'd enjoy, keep going.



What We'd Love to See (Not Required, But It Helps)



· Experience managing 3+ Instagram accounts at the same time

· Caption writing and content posting experience

· DM management experience

· Short-form video editing (CapCut, Canva, Premiere)

· Competitor research and trend analysis

· Google Sheets reporting (tracking, formulas, daily logs)

· Understanding of how the Instagram algorithm works

· Experience working with accounts in niche or sensitive categories



What We Pay



$500 to $1,500+ USD per month, depending on your experience and how many accounts you can handle well. Raises and bonuses are performance-based and built into the role. We don't cap what you can earn. If you produce results, your compensation grows.



We are not looking for the cheapest hire. We are looking for the best one.



Where This Can Go



This isn't a dead-end position. The right person will take on more accounts, earn more, and eventually lead a team of account managers. That growth path is real. It's earned through performance, consistency, and trust, but it's there for anyone who wants it.



Several people on our current team started where you'd be starting. They stayed because the work is stable, the pay is fair, and the growth is real.



Who Should NOT Apply



· You're not available 6 to 8 hours a day, 5 to 7 days a week

· Your computer is old, slow, or unreliable

· Your internet drops regularly

· You're careless with details or forget things easily

· You want something casual, temporary, or low-responsibility

· You can't keep business information confidential



How to Apply



Fill out our application form carefully. We read every answer, and how you fill out this form tells us a lot about how you'd do the job.







If your application is vague, rushed, or incomplete, we won't move forward. If it's thoughtful and shows you actually read this post, you'll hear from us.

title: Floor Plan Quality Controller & Editor
description:
Full-time | 40 hours per week | 7:30 AM–4:00 PM ACDT | Adelaide, South Australia



Clique Creative is South Australia’s go-to for top-tier photography, videography, and marketing services. Our vision is to craft and inspire high-quality, consistent marketing solutions for property, people, and projects—while delivering a premium customer experience and fostering a fun, supportive workplace.



We’re looking for a highly skilled and detail-driven Floor Plan Quality Controller & Editor to join our vibrant team. This position is a work-from-home position, working 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday, 7:00AM to 3:00PM ACDT, in alignment with our production and scheduling needs. 



As the Floor Plan Quality Controller & Editor, you will play a central role in keeping our production pipeline running smoothly each day. A key component of your work involves quality controlling floor plans each day for accuracy, completing edits in Adobe Illustrator, forecasting workloads, and ensuring all outputs meet client and agency standards.  This is a very important part of our business and floor plan accuracy is paramount.



Key Responsibilities - Floor Plan Quality Control:

- Conduct daily checks on floor plans for accuracy, consistency, and compliance with standards.

- Complete edits in Adobe Illustrator software.

- Forecast upcoming floor plan workloads and assess resource needs.

- Identify, record, and communicate discrepancies or design issues.

- Maintain workflow efficiency to meet strict daily deadlines.



About You

You will succeed in this role if you:



- Have excellent English, written and verbal

- Have exceptional attention to detail.

- Have 3 years minimum experience in a similar position.

- Are confident using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.

- Are comfortable with Google Drive, Gmail, and Mac/iOS systems.

- Are highly organised and efficient with task prioritisation.

- Thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

- Enjoy interacting with clients and colleagues.

- Work well independently and as part of a supportive team.

- (Bonus) Have experience or interest in architecture, drafting, or design.



Why Join Clique Creative?



- Join a fun, supportive, and passionate creative team.

- Work in an environment that values quality, innovation, consistency, and teamwork.

- Enjoy a varied role with opportunities to grow your operational and creative skills.

- Be part of a well-regarded creative services company supporting property, people, and projects across South Australia.



How to Apply



If you’re ready to join a dynamic, quality-driven creative team, we’d love to hear from you.

 outlining why you’re the right fit for this role.

title: Automotive Operations & Dispatch Coordinator
description:
Key Responsibilities



1. Fleet & Retail Ops Coordination

*Own the estimate-to-approval-to-route workflow:

*Create and submit estimates

*Follow up for approval

*Ensure parts are sourced

*Finalize tech routes for the following day

*Track all jobs in progress and flag issues proactively

*Ensure routes are realistic and efficient; confirm techs are prepped and ready

*Communicate clearly with customers and fleet managers about job timing, status, and follow-ups

*Submit and close out fleet approvals in Element, LeasePlan, and AutoIntegrate

*Ensure invoices match approvals; follow up on outstanding invoices and missing payments



2. Scheduling & Productivity Tracking

*Maintain the daily technician schedule, making updates as needed

*Monitor tech productivity; flag idle time, reschedules, or delays

*Track time logs and coordinate with bookkeeper for payroll



3. Customer Communication

*Respond to incoming texts, WhatsApp messages, and system inquiries in a timely, professional manner

*Follow up with clients as needed to resolve open issues or gather job-related info

*Escalate concerns appropriately and promptly



4. Admin & Reporting

*Submit a detailed Daily Recap covering completed work, pending tasks, blockers, and KPIs

*Keep Trello and other tools up-to-date with accurate job and task statuses

*Track inventory needs and coordinate ordering of parts, oil, and supplies

*Monitor A/R and follow up on past-due fleet invoices



5. Marketing & Content Support

*Publish our monthly newsletter in MailChimp with content from marketing team

*Coordinate publishing of content to Instagram /
 / Google My Business

*Other light admin support as needed for the marketing team



What Success Looks Like

*All work orders, estimates, approvals, and tech routes are handled with zero dropped balls

*You identify blockers or problems before they become fires

*Communication is clear, timely, and professional — with both customers and internal team

*The business runs like a machine — nothing gets missed or delayed

*You take full ownership of your responsibilities and require very little follow-up



Who You Are

*A natural systems thinker who thrives on clarity, organization, and ownership

*Extremely proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to do

*Excellent at written and verbal English communication

*Comfortable in the automotive world and eager to learn the details

title: Steel Estimator/Project Coordinator
description:
Established steel contractor company looking for reliable and professional candidates that are willing to work 10+ hours/week as steel estimators/project coordinator for steel contractor. Knowledge of construction cost estimating is required. Salary rates will be based on knowledge, experience, and reliability. 



Read and interpret bid instructions and abide by deadlines for preparation of estimates.

Read and interpret construction drawings and specifications, specifically as it pertains to the metals scope. 

Prepare materials take off, estimate equipment needed, pricing, labor, and any other items needed to perform the scope of work.

Contact suppliers and obtain quotes as directed.

Commission shop drawings and engineered calculations.

Liaise between engineer and shop drawings vendors to ensure accurate shop drawings that abide by construction drawings. 

Submit RFIs and submittals to the prime contractor.  

Update and maintain organized and accurate database records of project documents.

Communicate clearly, promptly, and professionally in all formats 
 and phone). 

Proficiency in Bluebeam Revu, Excel/Google sheets, and CAD. 

Assist in the coordination of the projects as needed.

Order materials and coordinate deliveries with tea
mbers. 

Abide by flexible work hours if required by a project or assignment. 

Perform any additional duties and office work as required. 

English fluency required; Spanish fluency not required but definitely a plus.

title: Opinion Research Analyst
description:
Remote - Eastern Time Zone





FOR APPLICANTS WITH MARKET OR OPINION RESEARCH EXPERIENCE



Are you a passionate and detail-oriented researcher who wants to be a respected member of our team?



We are searching for candidates who:



Have exceptional English proofreading and editing skills for grammar, clarity, and style consistency



Have at least on year of professional market or opinion research experience.



Can verify and maintain data accuracy across all stages of the research process



Have an exceptional eye for detail



Exhibit strong initiative/bias to action



Can perform tasks in a fast-paced environment



Work effectively both independently and as part of a team



More about the position:



Quality control: Review all deliverables for grammar, spelling, and formatting accuracy; cross-check data sets to ensure consistency and integrity before client delivery

Quantitative research: Develop comprehensive written reports

Data collection: Program and test surveys to ensure accuracy, sample design, and survey distribution

Data processing and analysis: Analyze research results, code and analyze open-ended responses, work with SPSS or similar statistical software

Data visualization: Create visuals for reporting and dashboards (PowerPoint, Canva, Looker Studio, etc.)

Qualitative Research: Manage and analyze focus groups/in-depth interviews, coordinate recruiting, assist in reporting

Participate in client meetings by taking notes, identifying key action steps, understanding client needs, and making presentations



Required qualifications:



At least one year of market or opinion research experience

A bachelor’s degree or higher

Proven English proofreading and editing ability

Experience with data quality checks and error detection in research outputs

Strong communication skills



What we provide:



A supportive, inclusive, and collaborative work environment

Opportunities for professional growth and development

Company paid PhilHealth or HMO

13th month

Paid time off



***TO APPLY***

Send a LINK to your resume and LinkedIn

Also, send a note about why you are interested in this position.



Put Opinion Research Analyst in subject line

Use 5 apply points



I am Adam Probolsky. I will read your application personally.



This position is available immediately.



About Probolsky Research: We are a woman- and Latina-owned market and opinion research firm working for corporate, election, government, and nonprofit clients.

title: DTC Ecommerce Video Editor
description:
Howdy! We are looking for people with experience creating high-converting DTC advertisements for Meta. We don't want people who are still figuring it out, we want editors who understand exactly why an ad works and can build one from a script without hand-holding.



You'll receive scripts and turn them into polished, high-converting video ads across three formats: 



- VSL (video sales letters)

- UGC-style editing

- Direct product sales ads. 



You'll also be expected to source supplementary footage from the web and use AI tools like Higgsfield to generate realistic filler clips when needed, keeping the final product engaging from hook to CTA.



We are looking for:



- Proven experience editing high-converting Meta ads for DTC brands

- Deep understanding of what makes a paid ad actually work: hooks, pacing, visual hierarchy, and product showcase

- Comfortable sourcing B-roll and stock content

- Experience with generative AI tools such as Higgsfield and Elevenlabs

- Proficient in your editing software of choice. CapCut, Premiere, DaVinci, whatever gets the job done

- Highly responsive: we move fast and expect you to too. You must have WhatsApp and reply promptly during working hours

- Fluent English: you'll need to interpret scripts, understand messaging nuance, and communicate clearly with the team



Of course, you will be compensated fairly :)



We give massive bonuses for winning creatives: when your ad hits, we reward you for your work and effort properly.

We also provide a clear path to a senior creative role with an increased salary for editors who consistently deliver.

You will never be overworked. If there are any personal requests (like going to hang out with friends on a work day) or you need to take time off, we will respect it (just let us know beforehand!)



Begin your application with the word APPLE as your very first word. Include a Google Drive link to your portfolio with a few Meta ads you've edited. Keep your entire application to 5 sentences or less. Do not include a resume or link to external websites, those applications will be immediately denied.



Looking forwards to having you as a part of our team.

title: SEO, Content Creation and Ads Specialist
description:
Job Title: SEO, Content Creation and Ads Specialist

Employment Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Salary: Up to PHP 25,000–35,000/month + commission (depending on qualifications and interview outcomes)

About the Role

Vertex Group is an Australian NDIS-focused business portfolio operating various brands across provider management software, compliance consulting, participant support, and plan management. We are looking for an SEO Specialist with strong content writing skills to grow our organic presence across traditional search engines and AI-driven platforms.

This role goes beyond standard SEO execution. You will own keyword strategy, produce high-quality content, manage technical SEO, and build our visibility in AI-generated search results — including platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews. You will work across multiple brands within the NDIS sector, so understanding this space is a clear advantage.



? What You Will Do

SEO & Content Strategy

• Develop and execute SEO strategies that produce measurable organic growth across the Vertex Group brand portfolio.

• Create high-quality, NLP-friendly content aligned with target audience search intent and business goals.

• Build and maintain content frameworks, SOPs, and reusable execution templates.

• Optimise content for AI-generated search results through Answer Optimisation (AO) and Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) techniques.

• Map content to funnel stages, from awareness through to conversion.

Keyword Research & Search Intent

• Own keyword research and identify high-impact opportunities across all brands.

• Map search intent across traditional and AI-driven search journeys.

• Align keyword targeting with audience segments, including NDIS providers, participants, and plan managers.

Technical SEO

• Identify and resolve technical issues including indexation, crawlability, Core Web Vitals, and site performance.

• Conduct regular SEO audits using industry-standard tools.

• Collaborate with developers and the marketing team to implement technical fixes efficiently.

• Manage and optimise WordPress websites using Elementor or equivalent page builders.

Paid Ads & Performance Marketing

• Plan, launch, and optimise paid advertising campaigns across multiple platforms.

• Manage and optimise campaigns on LinkedIn Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads,
 Ads, and YouTube Ads.

• Conduct A/B testing on creatives, copy, and targeting strategies to improve ROI.

• Monitor ad performance metrics (CTR, CPC, CPA, ROAS) and provide actionable insights.

• Align paid campaigns with SEO and content strategies for full-funnel marketing impact.

? Social Media Post & Blog Content Creation (Added Section)

• Create engaging, brand-aligned social media content across
, LinkedIn, Instagram, and TikTok.

• Research and write high-quality blog articles optimised for SEO, user intent, and AI search visibility.

• Repurpose long-form content (blogs, SEO pages, whitepapers) into short-form social posts, reels, captions, and carousels.

• Develop consistent content calendars for each brand under the Vertex Group portfolio.

• Analyse social content performance and adjust strategies based on insights and trends.

• Ensure all social and blog content aligns with NDIS-specific audience behaviour and terminology.

Data, Reporting & Performance Tracking

• Track organic and paid performance using GA4, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, and other tools.

• Deliver clear, actionable reports on SEO performance, ad campaigns, and content effectiveness.

• Monitor performance across both traditional search and emerging AI search channels.

Backlink & Authority Building

• Build quality backlinks through competitor analysis, outreach, and community engagement (Quora, Reddit, industry forums).

• Conduct domain authority and link quality analysis.

• Identify scalable, repeatable link acquisition opportunities.

Trends & Continuous Improvement

• Stay current with SEO, GEO, paid ads trends, and AI search developments including LLM-based search and platform shifts across Google, TikTok, and Instagram.

• Filter signal from noise — focus on high-impact changes, not every algorithm update.



? Requirements

• 2–4 years of hands-on SEO experience with a proven record of driving organic growth.

• Strong content writing skills — you can produce clear, well-structured, optimised content independently.

• Experience in social media content creation and blog writing.

• Solid understanding of on-page, off-page, and technical SEO.

• Hands-on experience managing paid ads (LinkedIn Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads,
 Ads, and YouTube Ads).

• Experience with WordPress content management and optimisation.

• Hands-on experience with Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, GA4, and Google Search Console.

• Familiarity with GEO and AI-driven search platforms (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews).

• Strong analytical mindset — you translate data into clear next actions.

• Fluent in written and spoken English.

• Able to work independently and manage multiple projects in a remote setting.



? Preferred Qualifications

• Knowledge of the Australian NDIS (National Disability Insurance Scheme).

• Experience in a multi-brand environment.

• Active participation in Reddit, Quora, or niche community platforms.

• Familiarity with Answer Optimisation (AO) and structured content for AI citation.

• Experience with Elementor or similar WordPress page builders.

• Understanding of local SEO, particularly within Australian markets.



? A Note on NDIS Knowledge

The NDIS sector has its own language, compliance framework, and audience behaviour. Candidates who already understand terms like Support Coordination, Plan Management, NDIS registration, and the NDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission will move faster and create more relevant content from day one. This knowledge is not mandatory — but it is a strong differentiator.

title: Revenue Operations Manager

Los Angeles County, CA (Remote)

About Us



We are a rapidly growing private security company operating across California, providing professional guard services for commercial, residential, and construction sites.



Our business manages multiple active locations simultaneously, requiring tight coordination between operations, dispatch, billing, and client communication.



We are hiring a Revenue Operations Manager to take ownership of the systems that drive our business forward — from service delivery to cash collection.

Operations run smoothly

Clients are serviced properly

Work performed is billed accurately

Revenue is collected efficiently



You will act as the central operator connecting:



Field operations

Dispatch (including offshore team)

Accounting / invoicing

Sales pipeline

Client communication



This is a systems-driven role, not an administrative position.

No-shows

Escalate critical issues to leadership



Outputs:



Client health tracking

Monthly check-ins

Retention reporting

6. Process & Systems Development

Build and maintain SOPs across:

Operations

Dispatch

Billing

Client communication

Improve workflows and implement automation where possible



Outputs:



SOP library

Process improvements

You will be measured on:

Lead response time

Pipeline velocity



Client



Retention rate

Satisfaction

Qualifications

Strong understanding of:

Multi-site operations

Billing and AR processes

Workflow and systems management

Skills

Systems thinking and process building

Financial awareness (AR, billing, margins)

Strong organizational and analytical ability

Advanced proficiency in Google Sheets / Excel

CRM experience (Pipedrive or similar)

Clear and professional communication

Preferred

Experience in the security industry

Experience managing dispatch or scheduling teams

Experience working with offshore teams

Experience building SOPs from scratch

What This Role Is NOT

Not an administrative assistant role

Not a purely accounting role

Not a purely operations role



This is a cross-functional operator role with accountability

Performance-based bonuses tied to:

Operational efficiency

Client retention

Growth Opportunity



This role has a clear path to:

Who You Are



You are:



Highly organized and detail-oriented

Systems-driven (you build processes, not just complete tasks)

How to Apply

Final Note
